description: Expanded geography contract fixtures for the evidence-first revision. Preserves the original 7 cases (remapped to decision_state) and adds boundary cases. LIVE cases (run through model+gate). Pure-adversarial gate categories (conflicting records, rationale/verdict mismatch, high-conf-unsupported, low-conf-correct, invalid output) are covered by prose-audit/gate.test.mjs. Real Paratua excerpts are verbatim; synthetic:true cases are clearly-labeled contract fixtures (NOT from the manuscript; no prose was modified to create them).

provenance.notes: Subset for the revision pilot; adds whiticar. Re-derive from the map image before production use.

provenance.source_ref: map: NW coast, clifftop keep + harbor, exposed to the western sea; no river
map: 'Luthan Rise is HILLS, NOT mountains'; S of Cirshe; gnome-held
map: 'Luthan Mts - real mountains, far WEST'
map: 'open ground south of the Rise'
map: 'Putakta (SW coast)'; sea trade with Cirshe by a southward coastal route
map: 'Cirshe to the Moryn Mts is SOUTH, not east'
map: 'Fort Luthan on the Luthan Rise - the one land gate between Cirshe and the rest of Paratua'
map: 'Whiticar (dwarven, mining)', north-central inland - NOT coastal, not across any ocean
map + CLAUDE.md: coastal southward sea route
map: 'Great Western Road (Cirshe to Fort Luthan)'

location.excerpt: To the south, the Luthan Mountains rose in a long ridge that separated the settled lands from the deeper wilderness beyond.

cases.extracted_claim: The mountainous Luthan feature lies to the south of Cirshe (named 'Luthan Mountains').

authoritative_ref.record_value: The Luthan feature SOUTH of Cirshe is the Luthan Rise (hills). The Luthan Mts (mountains) lie far WEST.

location.excerpt: its trade running south by land to Calet and across the ocean to Putakta

cases.extracted_claim: Cirshe trade runs south by land to Calet AND 'across the ocean' to Putakta.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Calet is south by land (supported). Cirshe->Putakta is a southward COASTAL sea route, not an open-ocean crossing (the 'across the ocean' component is contradicted).

location.excerpt: the rolling country steepened into the first ridges of the Luthan Rise. The Great Western Road climbed there toward Fort Luthan ... skirting the garrison through the hills to the west

cases.extracted_claim: The Luthan crossing south of Cirshe is the Luthan Rise (hills), with the Great Western Road and Fort Luthan.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Luthan Rise: hills, S of Cirshe, Fort Luthan on it, Great Western Road. Affirmatively supported.

location.excerpt: up into the Luthan Hills, the long folded country the gnomes had held since before the Order fell

cases.extracted_claim: The Luthan feature is the Luthan Hills (hills), gnome-held.

authoritative_ref.record_value: 'Luthan Hills' is a canonical alias of the Luthan Rise (hills, gnome-held). Affirmatively supported.

location.excerpt: a broad, sloping avenue paved in river stone that descended through the merchant quarter toward the harbor ... the way water fills the hole left by a stone removed from a riverbed

cases.extracted_claim: The words 'river stone' (paving) and 'riverbed' (simile) appear in a Cirshe scene.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Cirshe has no river; these tokens are figurative/material, not a geographic river claim.

location.excerpt: Sea traffic from Putakta crossed to its docks

cases.extracted_claim: Sea traffic 'crossed' from Putakta to Cirshe's docks.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Coastal-south sea route. 'crossed' is ambiguous: idiomatic 'came over by sea' vs an open-water crossing. Source addresses the route but the reading is ambiguous.

location.excerpt: the tin that came down from the mines in the eastern Luthans

cases.extracted_claim: There are tin mines in the 'eastern Luthans'.

authoritative_ref.record_value: The map records tin from Luthan mines but does NOT pin the mines' east/west position; 'eastern Luthans' is neither confirmed nor refuted (source silent on this component).

location.excerpt: Cirshe sat on the coast between these features, exposed to the western sea and the winter storms that crossed it

cases.extracted_claim: Cirshe sits on the coast, exposed to the western sea.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Cirshe is a NW-coast city on the western sea. Affirmatively supported.

location.excerpt: Some two hundred miles lay between the city and the Luthan Rise alone, south across the kingdom's farmland ... Past the Luthans the land ran on south ... toward the high valleys of the Moryn Range.

cases.extracted_claim: From Cirshe the route runs south to the Luthan Rise and on south to the Moryn Range.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Cirshe->Luthan Rise->Moryn is southward (map: 'Cirshe to the Moryn Mts is SOUTH'). Affirmatively supported by qualitative bearings (no coordinates needed).

location.excerpt: The Great Western Road climbed there toward Fort Luthan, the one gate by land between Cirshe and the rest of Paratua

cases.extracted_claim: Fort Luthan is the one land gate between Cirshe and the rest of Paratua.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Map: 'Fort Luthan ... the one land gate between Cirshe and the rest of Paratua'. Affirmatively supported.

location.excerpt: [SYNTHETIC CONTRACT FIXTURE, not manuscript] The free city of Zarnuth lies two days east of Cirshe.

cases.extracted_claim: A city named Zarnuth lies two days east of Cirshe.

authoritative_ref.record_value: No place 'Zarnuth' exists on the map; the claim cannot be affirmatively supported (source silent on the invented place).

location.excerpt: [SYNTHETIC CONTRACT FIXTURE, not manuscript] Fort Luthan guards the harbor at Putakta.

cases.extracted_claim: Fort Luthan is located at the harbor of Putakta.

authoritative_ref.record_value: Fort Luthan is on the inland Luthan Rise (hills); Putakta is a separate SW-coast city. Placing Fort Luthan at Putakta's harbor is contradicted.

location.excerpt: [SYNTHETIC CONTRACT FIXTURE, not manuscript] They climbed the Luthans, the great southern peaks.

cases.extracted_claim: The 'Luthans' are 'southern peaks' (mountains, to the south).

authoritative_ref.record_value: 'the Luthans' is a shared alias: the Luthan Rise (hills, SOUTH) and the Luthan Mts (mountains, WEST). 'southern' fits the Rise; 'peaks/mountains' fits the Mts. The alias cannot be disambiguated -> ambiguous.
